# Recipe: DevOps Work

> *"Get the rack humming and keep the park online."*

Use this when the work is operational delivery, not product behavior: CI/CD, build pipelines, deploy automation, monitoring, observability, release plumbing, or health-check wiring.

If the work is cross-cutting and not owned by a single feature, track it under `specs/_ops/OPS-*` instead of forcing it into a feature packet.

If the work needs a dedicated operational execution lane, use `bubbles.devops` or the `devops-to-doc` workflow mode.

## Focused DevOps Execution

```
/bubbles.workflow implement target:devops finalize:docs for 042-catalog-assistant

/bubbles.workflow  specs/_ops/OPS-001-ci-hardening mode: devops-to-doc
```

Use when:
- CI is failing but product behavior is otherwise understood
- deployment manifests or release automation need fixes
- monitoring, alerting, or observability wiring needs implementation
- build/release reproducibility needs operational changes

## Direct Specialist Use

```
/bubbles.devops  specs/042-catalog-assistant focus: deploy
/bubbles.devops  specs/042-catalog-assistant focus: monitoring
/bubbles.devops  specs/042-catalog-assistant focus: ci-cd
/bubbles.devops  specs/_ops/OPS-001-ci-hardening focus: ci-cd
```

## Focus Values

`bubbles.devops` accepts these `focus:` values. Pick the one closest to the work:

| Focus | Use When |
|-------|----------|
| `ci-cd` | GitHub Actions / GitLab CI / Jenkins workflow changes |
| `build` | Dockerfile changes, build script changes, build cache strategy |
| `deploy` | Deployment scripts that don't fit the per-target adapter model |
| `monitoring` | Prometheus/Grafana/alert rule changes |
| `observability` | OpenTelemetry, tracing, structured logging wiring |
| `release` | Release packet / change-log automation |
| `ops` | Generic operational glue that doesn't fit elsewhere |
| `deployment-target` | Per-target adapter authoring under `deploy/<target>/` |
| `config-sst` | Single-source-of-truth config + generated env/Compose/bundle work |
| `docker-lifecycle` | Cleanup policy, freshness verification, persistent volume safety |
| `docker-ports` | Port allocation (10k Rule) + Dual-URL Standard audits |
| `test-isolation` | Ephemeral test stack + disposable storage work |
| `release-automation` | `scripts/deploy/promote.sh`, `scripts/deploy/rollback.sh`, build manifest plumbing |

## Build-Once Deploy-Many Work

When the project ships images to multiple environments and Gate **G081** is in scope, every change MUST keep these invariants intact:

| Invariant | What |
|-----------|------|
| Digest pinning | Deployment manifests pin images by `sha256:<digest>`, never mutable tags |
| CI stops at registry push | No `ssh`/`scp`/`rsync`/`apply.sh` from CI |
| Cosign verification before run | Adapter `apply.sh` calls `cosign verify` against Sigstore + Rekor before container start |
| Bundle hash verification | Adapter verifies `sha256sum` of pulled config bundle against expected hash |
| Pointer-swap rollback | `rollback.sh` writes the prior `manifest.yaml` pointer pair, never rebuilds |
| Bundle is a build artifact | Generated by CI, immutable per `(env, sourceSha)`; never deploy-time generated |
| Per-target manifest | Each target owns its own `deploy/<target>/manifest.yaml` |

For new deployment-target work, follow the per-target adapter layout in the [`bubbles-deployment-target-adapter`](../../skills/bubbles-deployment-target-adapter/SKILL.md) skill. See also the dedicated [Build-Once Deploy-Many recipe](build-once-deploy-many.md).

## Companion Skills & Instructions

| Asset | When To Reference |
|-------|-------------------|
| [`bubbles-deployment-target-adapter`](../../skills/bubbles-deployment-target-adapter/SKILL.md) | Authoring or modifying any `deploy/<target>/` adapter |
| [`bubbles-config-sst`](../../skills/bubbles-config-sst/SKILL.md) | Adding config values, ports, env keys; touching generated artifacts |
| [`bubbles-docker-lifecycle-governance`](../../skills/bubbles-docker-lifecycle-governance/SKILL.md) | Cleanup policy, freshness verification, volume protection |
| [`bubbles-docker-port-standards`](../../skills/bubbles-docker-port-standards/SKILL.md) | Port allocation, Compose port mappings, Dual-URL audits |
| [`bubbles-test-environment-isolation`](../../skills/bubbles-test-environment-isolation/SKILL.md) | Test stack design, ephemeral storage, profile isolation |
| [`bubbles-deployment-target.instructions.md`](../../instructions/bubbles-deployment-target.instructions.md) | Companion enforcement instructions for deployment targets |
| [`bubbles-docker-lifecycle-governance.instructions.md`](../../instructions/bubbles-docker-lifecycle-governance.instructions.md) | Companion enforcement for Docker lifecycle |
| [`bubbles-config-sst.instructions.md`](../../instructions/bubbles-config-sst.instructions.md) | Companion enforcement for config SST |
| [`bubbles-test-environment-isolation.instructions.md`](../../instructions/bubbles-test-environment-isolation.instructions.md) | Companion enforcement for test isolation |

## Ops Packet Shape

For cross-cutting ops work, keep the packet under `specs/_ops/OPS-*` with:

- `objective.md`
- `design.md`
- `scopes.md`
- `runbook.md`
- `report.md`
- `state.json`

## Stability First, Then DevOps

If you know there are operational symptoms but not the root cause, start with the stability diagnostic lane:

```
/bubbles.workflow validate action:stability-diagnostic-then-devops finalize:docs for 042-catalog-assistant
```

That path now diagnoses with `bubbles.stabilize`, then routes operational execution through `bubbles.devops` before the rest of the quality chain runs.

## Not The Same As Framework Ops

Use [Framework Ops](framework-ops.md) when the problem is about Bubbles itself: hooks, gates, upgrades, framework health, or control-plane behavior.

Use this recipe when the problem is inside the target project's operational delivery surfaces.
